author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "‘Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.’

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: You therefore, not being meek, have lost both heaven and earth alike."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: Again and again you may hear the Lord saying, "Ye shall be witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judea, and in Samaria, and even in the whole earth."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: How is it, then, that those men have not lost heaven and earth, who, in order to avoid communicating with all the nations of the earth, despise the words of Him that sitteth in heaven?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For, in proof of your meekness, it is not your words but the cudgels of the Circumcelliones which should be examined.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: You will say, What has that to do with us?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Just as though we were making the remark with any other object except to extract that answer from you.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For the reason that your schism is a valid charge against you is that you do not allow that you are chargeable with another’s sin, whereas you have separated from us for no other reason but that you charge us with the sins of other men.
